title: Bridge Essay: A transformation from vision to imagery in the human brain.
concept: Mental imagery as real perception — imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal Reconstruction** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Imaginal — mental imagery as real perception, imagination activating sensory cortices, aphantasia.
**Phase context:** Mental imagery as real perception — imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.

**A transformation from vision to imagery in the human brain.** — bioRxiv (2025). DOI: 10.1101/2025.09.02.672180. PMID: 40950062. Extensive work has shown that the visual cortex is reactivated during mental imagery, and that models trained on visual data can predict imagery activity and decode imagined stimuli. These findings may explain why imagery can feel and function like vision, but give little insight into how the brain activity patterns that encode seen and mental images differ.
